title: ETL: CSV Ingest, Clean, Aggregate
goal: Load raw sales rows, clean missing values, and produce a regional KPI table.
steps: Read raw CSV with typed columns and parsed dates.
Normalize numeric fields and fill known missing values.
Aggregate totals and counts by region for reporting.
code:
import pandas as pd
from io import StringIO
raw = StringIO('region,order_date,revenue\nN,2026-03-01,120\nS,2026-03-01,90\nN,2026-03-02,')
df = pd.read_csv(raw, parse_dates=['order_date'])
df['revenue'] = pd.to_numeric(df['revenue'], errors='coerce').fillna(0)
summary = df.groupby('region', as_index=False).agg(total_revenue=('revenue','sum'), orders=('region','count'))
print(summary)
outputHint: 2-column KPI table by region with total_revenue and orders.

title: EDA: Distribution and Outlier Pass
goal: Understand metric distribution and identify suspicious extremes.
steps: Compute summary stats and percentile thresholds.
Plot histogram and boxplot for shape/outlier context.
Flag rows above p95 threshold for manual review.
code:
import pandas as pd, seaborn as sns, matplotlib.pyplot as plt
df = pd.DataFrame({'order_value':[25,30,35,45,60,62,70,250]})
p95 = df['order_value'].quantile(0.95)
fig, ax = plt.subplots(1,2, figsize=(10,4))
sns.histplot(df, x='order_value', bins=6, ax=ax[0])
sns.boxplot(df, y='order_value', ax=ax[1])
flagged = df[df['order_value'] > p95]
print(flagged.shape)
outputHint: Histogram + boxplot and a small flagged subset (rows above p95).

title: Feature Engineering for Churn
goal: Build reusable churn features and prepare a model-ready matrix.
steps: Create ratio and recency features in pandas.
code:
import pandas as pd
from sklearn.compose import ColumnTransformer
from sklearn.preprocessing import OneHotEncoder, StandardScaler
df = pd.DataFrame({'tenure':[3,24,5,30], 'tickets':[4,1,3,0], 'plan':['basic','pro','basic','enterprise']})
df = df.assign(ticket_rate=df['tickets'] / df['tenure'])
pre = ColumnTransformer([('num', StandardScaler(), ['tenure','tickets','ticket_rate']), ('cat', OneHotEncoder(handle_unknown='ignore'), ['plan'])])
X = pre.fit_transform(df)
print(X.shape)
outputHint: Transformed matrix suitable for fitting sklearn estimators.

title: Model Train + Evaluate Loop
goal: Train a baseline classifier and evaluate with CV and AUC.
steps: Split data into train/test with stratification.
Fit baseline model inside a pipeline.
Measure CV accuracy and test-set ROC AUC.
code:
from sklearn.model_selection import train_test_split, cross_val_score
from sklearn.pipeline import Pipeline
from sklearn.preprocessing import StandardScaler
from sklearn.linear_model import LogisticRegression
from sklearn.metrics import roc_auc_score
X = [[3,15],[24,80],[5,20],[30,95],[7,25],[20,70]]
y = [1,0,1,0,1,0]
Xtr, Xte, ytr, yte = train_test_split(X, y, test_size=0.33, random_state=42, stratify=y)
pipe = Pipeline([('scale', StandardScaler()), ('model', LogisticRegression(max_iter=300))])
pipe.fit(Xtr, ytr)
cv = cross_val_score(pipe, X, y, cv=3, scoring='accuracy')
auc = roc_auc_score(yte, pipe.predict_proba(Xte)[:,1])
print(cv.mean(), round(float(auc), 3))
outputHint: Mean CV score and AUC summary for baseline reporting.

title: Weekly Time-Series KPI
goal: Build weekly KPI series from event-level transactions and plot trend.
code:
import pandas as pd, seaborn as sns, matplotlib.pyplot as plt
df = pd.DataFrame({'ts':['2026-01-01','2026-01-02','2026-01-08'], 'gmv':[120,80,200]})
df['ts'] = pd.to_datetime(df['ts'])
weekly = df.resample('W', on='ts').agg(total_gmv=('gmv','sum')).reset_index()
ax = sns.lineplot(data=weekly, x='ts', y='total_gmv', marker='o')
ax.set_title('Weekly GMV')
plt.tight_layout()
print(weekly.shape)
outputHint: Weekly aggregated table and line chart showing trend direction.

title: Join Patterns: merge vs concat
goal: Understand when to use merge for key joins vs concat for stacking/appending.
steps: Use merge when combining by keys.
Use concat when appending aligned frames vertically or horizontally.
Validate row counts and key uniqueness after operation.
code:
import pandas as pd
orders = pd.DataFrame({'order_id':[1,2], 'customer_id':[10,20]})
customers = pd.DataFrame({'customer_id':[10,20], 'tier':['Pro','Basic']})
merged = orders.merge(customers, on='customer_id', how='left', validate='1:1')
part_a = pd.DataFrame({'order_id':[1], 'amount':[120]})
part_b = pd.DataFrame({'order_id':[2], 'amount':[90]})
stacked = pd.concat([part_a, part_b], ignore_index=True)
print(merged.shape, stacked.shape)
outputHint: Two outputs: key-based joined table and vertically stacked table.
